steps.title: Spot the concession
steps.content: The first sentence says signs with greater iconicity were more likely to be learned. The second sentence limits that finding by saying iconicity had a lower correlation with acquisition than sign frequency did. The needed transition must concede that the first finding is true while introducing a limiting comparison.

steps.title: Reject nonconcession signals
steps.content: A suggests emphasis, but the second sentence limits the prior point. B suggests restatement, but the sentence adds a comparison with sign frequency. D suggests cause and effect, but no result is being described. The answer is C.
